public long repairCars(int[] ranks, int cars) {

}

public long repairCars(int[] ranks, int cars) {

   long low = 1;

   long high = ... ;

   while (low < high) {

       long mid = (low + high) / 2;

       long sum = 0;

       for (int r : ranks) {

           sum += (long) Math.sqrt( mid / r );

       }

       if (sum >= cars) {

           high = mid;

       } else {

           low = mid + 1;

       }

   }

   return low;

}

In Java, for an array of integers, to find the minimum, we can use:

class Solution {
    public long repairCars(int[] ranks, int cars) {
        int minRank = Arrays.stream(ranks).min().getAsInt();
        long low = 1;
        long high = (long) minRank * (long) cars * (long) cars;
        
        while (low < high) {
            long mid = (low + high) / 2;
            long total = 0;
            for (int r : ranks) {
                long temp = (long) Math.sqrt(mid / r);
                total += temp;
                // To avoid overflow in case of very large ranks and cars?
                // Well, mid is up to 1e14, and temp is up to 1e6 (since cars is 1e6, mid is up to 1e14, r is 1, mid/r is 1e14, sqrt is 1e7. So for 1e5 elements, total is up to 1e5 * 1e7 = 1e12, which fits in long.
            }
            if (total >= cars) {
                high = mid;
            } else {
                low = mid + 1;
            }
        }
        return low;
    }
}

import java.util.Arrays;

class Solution {
    public long repairCars(int[] ranks, int cars) {
        int minRank = Arrays.stream(ranks).min().getAsInt();
        long low = 1;
        long high = (long) minRank * (long) cars * (long) cars;
        
        while (low < high) {
            long mid = (low + high) / 2;
            long total = 0;
            for (int r : ranks) {
                long temp = (long) Math.sqrt(mid / r);
                total += temp;
            }
            if (total >= cars) {
                high = mid;
            } else {
                low = mid + 1;
            }
        }
        return low;
    }
}
